title: Merge K Sorted Arrays
constraints: 1 <= K; total elements across arrays <= 2e5; arrays sorted non-decreasing
description: Given K sorted arrays, merge them into a single sorted array and print it.

solutions.python:
import sys

K = int(sys.stdin.readline())

all_elements = []

for _ in range(K):
    line = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split()))
    n = line[0]
    elements = line[1:n+1]
    all_elements.extend(elements)

all_elements.sort()

print(*all_elements)

starter_code.python:
import sys, heapq
k=int(sys.stdin.readline())
h=[]; arrays=[]
for i in range(k):
    parts=list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split()))
    n=parts[0]
    arr=parts[1:]
    arrays.append(arr)
    if n>0:
        heapq.heappush(h,(arr[0],i,1))
res=[]
while h:
    v,i,j=heapq.heappop(h)
    res.append(v)
    if j<=len(arrays[i]):
        heapq.heappush(h,(arrays[i][j],i,j+1))
print(" ".join(map(str,res)))
